title: Scott Pelley on the Bari Weiss Era and His Last Days at ‘60 Minutes’
summary: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an exclusive interview with the now-former CBS News correspondent.

title: 5 Takeaways From Scott Pelley’s Times Magazine Interview
summary: Mr. Pelley, who was at CBS News for 37 years, including as a White House correspondent and a “60 Minutes” correspondent, spoke in his first extended interview since he was fired.

title: ‘60 Minutes’ Is a ‘Cage Full of Tigers.’ Can Nick Bilton Lead It?
summary: The tech journalist and filmmaker was a surprise choice to lead a famously combative newsroom. His first week was chaos.

title: RFK Jr. Appears Disengaged on Many Health Department Matters Beyond Vaccines
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has demonstrated little interest in managing his sprawling department as he focuses on food and vaccine policies, according to colleagues.

title: Lawsuit Aims to Stop U.F.C. Fights at White House on Trump’s Birthday
summary: A federal lawsuit said the event, set for June 14, was unlawfully planned and designed to benefit Mr. Trump and his allies.

title: Hegseth Criticizes Europe Over Migration ‘Invasion’ in D-Day Speech
summary: The remarks from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reflect many of the Trump administration’s previous assertions on immigrants in Europe, which overlap with the language of European far-right political parties.

title: Iranians Sink Into Despair Facing War Deaths and Skyrocketing Inflation
summary: An imploding economy is causing hopelessness among both pro- and anti-government Iranians. And for those who wished for regime change, the letdown is palpable.

title: Trump Says Iran Has Made a ‘Big’ Nuclear Promise. It Isn’t New.
summary: President Trump’s boasts of securing a commitment from Iranian leaders not to develop a nuclear weapon have puzzled nuclear experts who note that Tehran has made that pledge for more than 50 years.

title: Israel Bombs Beirut Outskirts as Fighting With Hezbollah Escalates
summary: U.S. efforts for a truce in Lebanon appear to have stalled. Israel accused Hezbollah of firing at Israeli territory. Iran threatened to retaliate.

title: Democrats in Congress Grapple With Concerns Over Platner Allegations
summary: Representative Ro Khanna said that he believed the account of a woman who accused Graham Platner of physically threatening behavior, and urged his campaign not to criticize her.

title: Knicks Watch Party at MSG Is Canceled as Game 3 Security Ramps Up for Trump
summary: President Trump and Mayor Zohran Mamdani are expected to attend the game on Monday at Madison Square Garden. A viewing party on Friday led to more than a dozen arrests.

summary: Villanova University alumni have been grabbing headlines, both on the court and in the Vatican.

title: 2026 Tony Awards: What to Expect
summary: This year’s annual celebration of the best on Broadway is being hosted by Pink.

title: June Squibb, the ‘Foulest Mouth on Broadway’, Is Up for a Tony at 96
summary: At 96, June Squibb is one of the oldest acting nominees in the history of the Tony Awards. Back in the 1960s, she was known for an entirely different distinction.

title: How to Watch the 2026 Tony Awards
summary: The ceremony, at Radio City Music Hall, will be broadcast on CBS starting at 8 p.m. Eastern, and livestreamed on Paramount+ for premium subscribers.

title: Seattle’s Incredibly Loud Street Preachers Eagerly Await the World Cup
summary: Long the bane of Mariners and Seahawks fans, evangelical ministers with large speakers are preparing for the “incredible opportunity” of screaming the word of God at 750,000 soccer fans.

summary: The nearly 100-year-old airport has undergone a rapid $500 million renovation. But experts say it’s not enough.

summary: Hollywood is struggling, but Spielberg insists that the big screen is still the best place to work out our collective dreams, joys and sorrows.

title: You Can Walk to the World Cup in New Jersey. But Should You?
summary: We decided to make the trek and find out.

title: Who Will Account for Another Foreign Policy Failure?
summary: The American foreign policy class should shed its addiction to military force.

title: Touching Grass
summary: There’s no better time than early summer to put away your phone and get some fresh air. We have a challenge to help you actually do it.

title: Christine Hunsicker, CaaStle CEO, Confessed to Fraud. The Board Let Her Stay in Charge.
summary: The final months of the fashion tech start-up CaaStle included fake audits, stock buybacks and a damage control strategy led by the chief executive who defrauded it.
